# Kith

> Serverless, end-to-end-encrypted, no-account sync for your own devices — memory,
> tabs, and files. Direct device-to-device over iroh (QUIC) with Automerge CRDTs; no
> cloud and no server that can read your data. Also an MCP server, so AI assistants can
> use your memory/tabs/files locally. Desktop app for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

## Key facts
- Apps: Memory (notes/facts), Tabs (links), Files (E2E file transfer), Spaces (N isolated encrypted worlds; Team spaces add per-device Admin/Writer/Reader roles, epoch-key revocation, and a tamper-evident audit log), Devices (pairing), Agents (MCP).
- Pairing: a short one-time code (SPAKE2); no account. Sync and file transfer are gated by a mutual group-key proof and, in Team spaces, by EndpointId membership — a non-member is refused even with a leaked key.
- MCP: run `kith serve` (the same binary, bound to one space) — tools: memory.append/search/read/forget, tabs.add/list/forget, files.share/list/fetch/read/search.
- Recovery: no account, so back up via an encrypted, passphrase-protected Space export/import. Keys can live in the OS keychain; a self-hosted relay/discovery is optional (Infra::SelfHosted).
- License: MIT OR Apache-2.0. Status: alpha (not independently audited).

## Links
- Source: https://github.com/muhamadjawdatsalemalakoum/kith
- Download: https://github.com/muhamadjawdatsalemalakoum/kith/releases
- Author: Muhamad J. Akoum (https://www.linkedin.com/in/akoum/)
